summary: Rootline Defense now has its first control plant. Frost Fern costs 65 sap, deals no damage and generates no sap; it chills a 3-column lane zone in front of it, slowing enemies by 40% and cutting their attack rate by 25% for 2.5s. A reusable, typed status-effect system underpins the slow and opens the archetype for future control plants.

candidates.title: Frost Fern — First Control Plant and Reusable Status-Effect System
candidates.summary: Adds Frost Fern as a non-damaging, non-sap control plant that chills a 3-column lane zone for 2.5s; ships a reusable status-effect system on enemies; routes move/attack cadence through effective-speed/cadence helpers; adds three-layer slow visuals, a chill-zone hover preview, a Board Scout Control chip with a six-field detail panel, and the April 17 Cold Lane scenario with a two-wave tutorial, a four-wave 1-HP challenge, and an April 16 endless inheritance.

keep: Control is a third first-class role alongside attacker and support — the game now has three distinct plant verbs.
Chill is observable: cool-blue tint, a following frost-particle emitter, and a visibly slower walk animation on chilled enemies.
Board Scout's six-field detail panel (Cost / AoE / Slow / Attack Slow / Duration / Notes) spells out the mechanic before the player spends sap.
mustChange: Playwright must lock the no-stack refresh rule so two Frost Ferns do not silently double-slow.
risks: The 40% speed + 25% attack-rate slow is a big lever on Glass Ram; tuning drift could make rams trivial.

keep: The typed status-effect map + effective-speed/cadence helpers are the reusable foundation future control plants (burn, stun, root) need.
Routing walker move, walker contact cadence, and the full sniper FSM through the helpers makes slow composable rather than one-off.
The scenario teaches Frost Fern in a two-wave tutorial: Wave 1 proves Thorn Vine alone cannot hold, Wave 2 introduces Frost Fern so the shift is felt.
mustChange: The script role-heuristics in probe/validate/bot must exclude control alongside support so Frost Fern is never selected as the primary attacker.
risks: Cadence-helper refactor drift: five call sites must keep prior-day scenario timing intact — regression specs carry the weight.

keep: The chill-zone hover preview turns an invisible AoE into a tile-accurate rectangle before commit.
Manifest-backed SVG art for both frost-fern and frost-particle keeps the daily screenshot crisp and asset-traceable.
Two paired replay fixtures (no-control → gameover, chilled-lane → cleared) give the runtime probe a deterministic must-not-regress signal.
mustChange: The frost-particle emitter must be guarded by typeof emitter.startFollow === 'function' and try/catch so older Phaser versions fall back to the tint + frame-rate layers.
risks: Chill too weak is as dangerous as chill too strong: if a slowed sniper's aim line barely changes, the mechanic fails the observable bar.

keep: Ship a real control plant with a reusable, typed status-effect system that future burn/stun/root plants will share.
Teach the role in a two-wave tutorial (Hold the Lane → Now It's Too Fast) before the 1-HP challenge.
Add a chill-zone hover preview, a Board Scout Control chip, and a six-field detail panel so the mechanic is visible before commit.
mustChange: Helpers applyStatusEffect/tickStatusEffects/getEffectiveSpeed/getEffectiveCadence must be exported from play.js so Playwright can lock the math without scene state.
updateControlPlants must apply chill as a zone (x in [fern.x − CELL_WIDTH/2, fern.x − CELL_WIDTH/2 + 3*CELL_WIDTH]) rather than per-enemy overlap probing.
Script role-heuristics in probe/validate/bot must exclude role === 'control' alongside role === 'support' from primary-attacker selection.
risks: Balance drift on chill magnitude could make Glass Ram trivial or leave chilled snipers indistinguishable from unchilled ones.
Cadence-helper refactor must not shift prior-day scenario timing — April 13/14/15/16 regression coverage is required before publish.
feedbackReferences: Players asked for more plant roles beyond attacker/support; control is the third first-class role.
Board Scout feedback pushed for observable pre-run mechanic copy, now extended with a Control chip and a six-field detail panel.

candidates.title: Second Ranged Enemy: Thorn Mortar
candidates.summary: Adds a second ranged enemy that lobs a slow arcing projectile instead of a straight bolt. Deepens ranged threat but reuses the sniper archetype without introducing a plant role.
keep: Low implementation risk relative to a new plant role.
mustChange: Needs a new strategic verb beyond 'same as sniper, arcing'.
risks: Adds threat variety without expanding the plant roster.

candidates.title: Sap-Cap UI Pass
candidates.summary: Adds a visible sap cap and overflow indicator. Improves resource legibility but does not open a new gameplay system.
keep: Low risk; strictly a UI clarity improvement.
mustChange: Needs a visible daily hook beyond a meter update.
risks: Hard to summarize in a social post.

winner.title: Frost Fern — First Control Plant and Reusable Status-Effect System
winner.summary: Adds Frost Fern as a non-damaging, non-sap control plant that chills a 3-column lane zone for 2.5s; ships a reusable status-effect system on enemies; routes move/attack cadence through effective-speed/cadence helpers; adds three-layer slow visuals, a chill-zone hover preview, a Board Scout Control chip with a six-field detail panel, and the April 17 Cold Lane scenario with a two-wave tutorial, a four-wave 1-HP challenge, and an April 16 endless inheritance.

rationale: Frost Fern won because it makes damage and control two separate levers in Rootline Defense for the first time. The work is load-bearing: a typed status-effect system, effective-speed/cadence helpers that replace five raw reads, a zone-apply control pattern, three-layer slow visuals, Board Scout control rendering, and a script role-heuristic update all become reusable foundations for future control plants (burn, stun, root). Reference commits: a68683f (task_1 runtime/scenario/assets), b1e8c24 (task_2 Playwright specs + replay fixtures).

feedbackInfluence.summary: Players asked for plant roles beyond attacker and support; Frost Fern adds control as the third first-class role.
Board Scout feedback pushed for observable pre-run mechanic copy — the Control chip and six-field detail panel extend that pattern.

keyDecisions: Model Frost Fern as role: "control" with cost 65, hp 28, cadenceMs 400, chillRangeCols 3, chillMagnitude 0.4, chillAttackMagnitude 0.25, chillDurationMs 2500; no projectile, no sap pulse.

Export applyStatusEffect, tickStatusEffects, getEffectiveSpeed, and getEffectiveCadence from play.js and route walker move, walker contact cadence, sniper approach, sniper aim-init, and sniper cooldown refill through them.
Apply chill as a lane zone [fern.x − CELL_WIDTH/2, fern.x − CELL_WIDTH/2 + 3*CELL_WIDTH] on the fern's row; do not stack magnitudes — merge by max-of-magnitudes + latest expiry.

Render three slow layers per enemy: tint 0x8fd8ff (MULTIPLY mode reset before re-apply), a frost-particle emitter attached via startFollow (try/catch guarded), and frame-rate scaled by (1 − slow.magnitude).
Expose per-enemy baseSpeed/effectiveSpeed/statusEffects and per-control-defender aoeShape/aoeRangeCols/chill* in the observation API for deterministic Playwright + probe assertions.

Board Scout renders a .game-scout__badge--control chip and a six-field detail panel (Cost, AoE, Slow, Attack Slow, Duration, Notes) with the exact values 65 / 3-col lane zone / 40% speed / 25% attack rate / 2.5s / No damage, no sap; refreshes on re-chill (no stack).
Ship scenario 2026-04-17.js with a two-wave tutorial (Hold the Lane → Now It's Too Fast), a four-wave 1-HP challenge, and endless inherited from April 16.
Update script role-heuristics in probe-runtime-scenario.mjs, validate-scenario-difficulty.mjs, and bot-play-scenario.mjs to exclude role === "control" alongside role === "support".
headline: Frost Fern brings control play to Rootline Defense

bluesky_post.headline: Frost Fern brings control play to Rootline Defense
bluesky_post.body: Day 12 adds the first plant that does not damage and does not pay sap — it chills a 3-column lane zone, slowing enemies 40% and cutting their attack rate 25% for 2.5s. Damage and control are finally two separate levers.
bluesky_post.alt_text: Rootline Defense game page showing the Frost Fern plant card with a Control chip and a cool-blue chill zone over three tiles of a lane.

replyTemplates.whatIs: Command Garden ships one visible website change per day. Today's change adds Frost Fern, a control plant that chills a lane zone instead of attacking.
replyTemplates.howDoesItWork: Frost Fern costs 65 sap, deals no damage, and chills a 3-column lane zone in front of it for 2.5s — enemies inside are 40% slower and fire 25% slower. Refreshes on re-chill; does not stack.
replyTemplates.genericQuestion: The full decision trail and test notes are published with the day artifact so the feature can be inspected end to end.

bluesky_strategy.growthNotes: Control-role posts invite game-design discussion; lead with the chill zone gif and the damage-vs-control framing.
